_meta.do_not_edit: Edit the per-domain source files; CI regenerates this file.

content.markdown:
# Chat with AI Steward

The AI steward panel surfaces AI-generated insights in Studio and Explorer. Its copy rules make generated content trustworthy: cited, confidence-labeled, and clearly distinguished from static UI.

---

## Voice

The steward explains, it does not perform. Plain sentences, no exclamation marks, no anthropomorphic filler ("I think…", "Let me…"). Answers lead with the finding, then the evidence.

## Source attribution (citation pattern)

- Every AI-generated insight carries a source line: `Source: <asset name>`, linking to the catalog asset it was derived from.
- Multiple sources: comma-separated, max 3 visible, "+N more" beyond that.
- Never present an uncited insight as fact; uncited output is labeled "Generated — verify before use."

## Confidence

- Confidence is a labeled value, not a bare number: `Confidence: High` / `Medium` / `Low` (badge component). Show the percentage in a tooltip, not inline.
- Low-confidence answers must include the verify affordance ("Check source").

## Disclaimer

- One persistent, plain-language line in the panel footer: "AI-generated content can contain errors. Verify important information."
- Per app-context vocabulary: the feature is "Ask AI" — never "chatbot", "AI search", or "assistant".

## Actions

- Output actions use verb-first labels: "Accept", "Regenerate", "Discard" (matches the accessibility checklist's required affordances).

The steward explains, it does not perform. Plain sentences, no exclamation marks, no anthropomorphic filler ("I think…", "Let me…"). Answers lead with the finding, then the evidence.

Low-confidence answers must include the verify affordance ("Check source").
One persistent, plain-language line in the panel footer: "AI-generated content can contain errors. Verify important information."

Output actions use verb-first labels: "Accept", "Regenerate", "Discard" (matches the accessibility checklist's required affordances).

usage.markdown:
## Where it lives

The steward is the AI surface for **Studio and Explorer**. It is not part of Administration. It opens as a side panel (Drawer variant) over the working context — search results, asset detail, lineage — and never navigates away.

## When AI content appears

- On explicit user ask ("Ask AI" entry points: global header action, asset detail, search results).
- As inline suggestions ("Suggestion" = AI-generated metadata enrichment) surfaced in context; suggestions follow the same identification rules as panel content and are always dismissible.

## Distinguishing AI content from static UI

1. **Surface:** AI content renders on an elevated surface (see the design guidelines) — never flush with static page content.
2. **Identification:** every AI-generated block carries the sparkle marker (`ai` icon) with the accessible label "Generated by AI". Identification is icon + label, never color alone.
3. **Provenance:** source line + confidence (see the content guidelines). Static UI never carries these; their presence is itself a signal.

## Interaction expectations

- Accept / Regenerate / Discard on every output; generation is cancellable.
- AI output is never injected into the user's focus path without warning.
- Suggestions are bypassable — they must never gate task completion.

## Don't

- Don't use the steward pattern in Administration (no AI surface there today).
- Don't restyle static components in "AI purple" to imply intelligence.
- Don't stream into a surface the user is actively editing.

design.markdown:
## Surface elevation

The panel is an overlay surface: background `--zen-color-bg-default`, border `--zen-border-default`, shadow `--zen-shadow-xl` (overlay tier per the foundations elevation scale; cards within the panel use `--zen-shadow-xs`). Elevation signals depth, not decoration — one elevated tier, no stacking.

## Identification

- Panel header: `ai` icon (sparkle) + "AI Steward" title.
- Per-insight marker: `ai` icon at 16px with accessible label "Generated by AI".
- Identification is never color-only. The purple primitives (`--zen-color-purple-*`) MAY accent the marker but are not the marker.

## Confidence indicator

Badge component (Number type) next to the source line: High / Medium / Low. Colors follow standard badge semantics — no AI-specific color coding.

## Streaming state

- While generating: skeleton shimmer blocks (foundations spec: 2000ms linear loop — currently not tokenized; flagged for the motion-token program) in the answer area, `aria-busy="true"` on the region.
- Completion announced via `aria-live="polite"`.
- Shimmer and any typewriter effect respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (static placeholder instead).
- Motion durations/easings: use the foundations motion tokens (status 🟡 Proposed — cite, don't invent values).

## States

Closed (entry point visible) · Open (Drawer) · Generating (shimmer + Stop/Cancel) · Answered (insight + source + confidence + actions) · Error (alert-banner inside panel, retry affordance).

Elevation signals depth, not decoration — one elevated tier, no stacking.
